• RIC赢下了全部三场双打比赛，早早取得了3-0的领先优势。

Bridgewater, Massachusetts – On Saturday afternoon, the Rhode Island College men's tennis team decisively defeated Bridgewater State University 9-0 at the Rosen Tennis Courts, maintaining their undefeated streak in the Little East Conference.

Rhode Island College 9, Bridgewater State University 0
(RIC 6-1, 2-0 LEC)
(BSU 0-2, 0-2 LEC)

Match Progression
• RIC won all three doubles matches, taking an early 3-0 lead.
• Freshman Cam Chadwick (Narragansett, Rhode Island) and Mike Dickie (Smithfield, Rhode Island) defeated senior Michael Scollins (Mashfield, Massachusetts) and senior Dan O'Malley (Duxbury, Massachusetts) 8-0, securing RIC's first point of the day.
• When senior Brett Taggart (North Kingstown, Rhode Island) and freshman Alex Bourque (North Smithfield, Rhode Island) defeated senior Justin Haley (South Yarmouth, Massachusetts) and sophomore Joshua Bean (Kensington, Massachusetts) 8-2 in the first doubles match, the Anchormen extended their lead to 2-0.
• Senior Matthew Gambardella (West Warwick, Rhode Island) and freshman Ethan Hoy (West Warwick, Rhode Island) swept the doubles matches by defeating senior Jonathan Pereira (Forrest, Massachusetts) and senior Zachary Mutti (Pawtucket, Massachusetts) 8-3 in the third doubles match.
• Gambardella defeated Pereira in the fourth singles match with an impressive (6-0, 6-0) victory, giving RIC a 4-0 lead.
• Dickie defeated Bean in the sixth singles match (6-0, 6-1), securing the Anchormen's fifth and decisive point of the afternoon.
• Taggart defeated Scollins in the second singles match (6-2, 6-1), and Bourque defeated O'Malley in his match (6-1, 6-4), bringing RIC's score to 7-0.
• The Anchormen concluded the day's matches with Hoy defeating Mutti in the fifth singles match (6-4, 6-0), while Chadwick completed the sweep of the entire match by defeating Haley in the first singles match (6-1, 6-1).
• In the next match, Rhode Island College will host Lesley on Monday, April 9th, at 3:30 PM.

source_text:
在我生命中的不同阶段，观察力敏锐的陌生人仅通过看我手写的东西就能猜出我的职业。我能告诉你的只有两种可以实现这种技巧的职业。我先从简单的那个开始：如果字迹看起来像乱涂乱画且无法辨认，人们可能会猜作者是一名医生，因为太忙了而顾不上字形。然后就是建筑师。那么，揭示作者是建筑师的特征是什么？是什么让建筑师的字迹与他人不同？建筑学和手写字之间真的有联系吗？

出于某种我无法确定的原因，建筑师是在“绘字”而非“写字”。我怀疑这种语义上的差异仅仅是为了更强调实际字母的笔触和形状。人们经常观察到，建筑师倾向于使用“印刷体”而非“草书”。这似乎起源于所有建筑图纸和文字都由手工完成的时代，而印刷体方块字显然比草写体更容易阅读。由于建筑师的图纸被用于建造房屋，因此清晰易读至关重要。建筑师还倾向于全部使用大写字母（这并不总是被视为在尖叫！），同样是为了可读性。然而，最能区分建筑绘字特征的是其字形是风格化的。这里有一个小秘密（或者也许根本不是秘密）：建筑绘字并非“自然”产生。它是经过精心打造的。在 Doug Patt 的经典视频《如何像建筑师一样写字》中可以找到关于这个过程的极佳解释。

建筑师风格化的绘字与我们的同事——工程师的字迹形成对比。工程师在图纸和文字中追求一致性和统一性，而建筑师则在较宽松的限制下追求创造力和个性。通过考虑笔画的倾斜度、主干的垂直度、相似字符的形状、比例以及字符之间的间距，人们可以打造出一种个人绘字“风格”。在建筑师完全手工绘图和绘字的年代，人们仅凭绘字方式就能识别出哪些建筑师参与了哪些图纸。建筑师发展出了自己的绘图风格。Richard Neutra 开发了一种设计精良且极具辨识度的绘字风格，以至于被当代字体铸造厂 House Industries 所采用。这一类别的其他建筑师还包括 Frank Lloyd Wright 和 Charles Rennie Macintosh。讽刺的是，这种向字体的转换使得这些建筑师创造的文字变得既一致又统一。

建筑师在绘字中投入的设计考量也延伸到了其他领域。建筑师展现其手写灵巧度的另一种方式体现在他们的签名中。很多建筑师拥有非常酷的签名。无论字符是否可辨，这些签名通常都是精心打造的设计。Renzo Piano 和 Ralph Raison 是我最喜欢的两位。

因此，图纸、绘字和签名与建筑师的个人风格紧密相连。这种风格的最终体现形式就是一座建筑，而没有任何一种建筑类型比博物馆更令建筑师向往。这就是我们将要探讨的下一个建筑关联。请与我一起关注《艺术或建筑》。
translation:
In different stages of my life, strangers with keen observation skills could guess my profession just by looking at my handwriting. I can tell you there are only two professions that can achieve this skill. I'll start with the simpler one: if the handwriting looks like scribbles and is illegible, people might guess the author is a doctor who is too busy to worry about letter formation. Then there are architects. So, what are the characteristics that reveal the author is an architect? What makes an architect's handwriting different from others? Is there really a connection between architecture and handwriting?

For some reason I cannot determine, architects are "drawing" rather than "writing." I suspect this semantic difference is merely to emphasize the strokes and shapes of the actual letters. People often observe that architects tend to use "print" rather than "cursive." This seems to originate from the era when all architectural drawings and text were done by hand, and block print was clearly easier to read than cursive. Since architects' drawings are used to build houses, clarity and readability are crucial. Architects also tend to use all capital letters (which is not always seen as shouting!), also for readability. However, what most distinguishes the architectural drawing style is that its letterforms are stylized. Here is a little secret (or maybe not a secret at all): architectural drawing is not "naturally" produced. It is meticulously crafted. An excellent explanation of this process can be found in Doug Patt's classic video, "How to Write Like an Architect."

The stylized drawing of architects contrasts with the handwriting of our colleagues—engineers. Engineers pursue consistency and uniformity in drawings and text, while architects pursue creativity and individuality under looser constraints. By considering the slant of the strokes, the verticality of the main lines, the shape of similar characters, proportions, and the spacing between characters, one can develop a personal drawing "style." In the era when architects drew and wrote entirely by hand, people could identify which architects were involved in which drawings just by their drawing style. Architects developed their own drawing styles. Richard Neutra developed a well-designed and highly recognizable drawing style that was adopted by the contemporary type foundry House Industries. Other architects in this category include Frank Lloyd Wright and Charles Rennie Mackintosh. Ironically, this transition to typography made the text created by these architects both consistent and unified.

The design considerations an architect puts into drawing also extend to other areas. Another way architects demonstrate their manual dexterity is in their signatures. Many architects have very cool signatures. Regardless of whether the characters are legible, these signatures are usually carefully designed. Renzo Piano and Ralph Raison are my favorites.

Therefore, drawings, drawing, and signatures are closely linked to an architect's personal style. The ultimate manifestation of this style is a building, and no building type is more desired by architects than a museum. This is the next architectural connection we will explore. Please follow along with me on "Art or Architecture."

美国支持的库尔德和阿拉伯战士在叙利亚北部开辟了对抗伊斯兰国（IS）组织的新战线。
报道称，叙利亚民主力量（SDF）正向土耳其边境附近的IS控制城镇曼比季推进，旨在切断该极端组织用于跨境运送物资和外国战士的区域。
总部位于英国的叙利亚人权观察组织6月1日表示，SDF部队已控制16个村庄，距离曼比季约15公里。
该组织还表示，由美国领导的国际联盟在黎明前对曼比季发动的空袭造成15名平民死亡。
SDF顾问纳赛尔·哈吉·曼苏尔（Nasser Haj Mansour）表示，冲突“激烈且猛烈”。
据引述匿名美国官员的话称，此次行动部署了数千名部队，可能会持续数周。
他们表示，少数美国特种作战部队将作为顾问在地面支持此次推进。
基于法新社（AFP）、路透社（Reuters）和美联社（AP）的报道。
translation:
Kurdish and Arab fighters supported by the United States have opened a new front against the Islamic State (IS) organization in northern Syria.
Reports say the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) are advancing toward Manbij, an IS-controlled town near the Turkish border, aiming to cut off the area used by the extremist group for cross-border transport of supplies and foreign fighters.
The UK-based Syrian Observatory for Human Rights stated on June 1 that SDF forces have controlled 16 villages, about 15 kilometers from Manbij.
The organization also reported that airstrikes launched before dawn on Manbij by the US-led international coalition killed 15 civilians.
SDF advisor Nasser Haj Mansour said the conflict was "fierce and intense."
According to anonymous US officials quoted, the operation has deployed thousands of troops and may last for several weeks.
They said a small number of US special operations forces will provide ground support as advisors for this advance.
Based on reports from AFP, Reuters, and AP.

2009年12月25日
当我们回顾过去十年（就此而言，也包括过去一年）新闻价值的衰减时，我们可能会看到重新定义什么是新闻的机会。这个想法源于美联社（AP）今天发布的关于华盛顿州年度头条新闻的报告。
换句话说，这些是全州范围内今年最大的、最具新闻价值的故事。（每个州，或者至少几乎所有州，每年都会汇总类似的清单。）
第一名是11月四名莱克伍德警察被枪击的事件。毫无疑问，这是一个强有力的、戏剧性的、令人心碎的故事；它确实是重大新闻。这件事有些不寻常，因为这次事件是对当时甚至没有在与公众互动的警察的伏击。
但世界，或者华盛顿州，或者其中的大部分，因此而改变了吗？我们学到了什么非常新东西吗？在这种情况下，一个疯子决定杀掉警察；我们大多数人可能都知道，（遗憾地，确实）这就是这个世界的现状。
第二个故事是选民批准了“除婚姻以外的一切法规”。那是该州的一次重大变革，标志着全国文化观念的显著转变。第三个故事是该州9%以上的失业率——这是一个巨大的新现实，正面对并影响着（直接或间接）所有华盛顿州居民。第四个故事是《西雅图邮报-情报报》（Seattle Post-Intelligencer）停止纸质运营——这是该州通信、新闻报道、政治和经济的一个重大转折点。第五个故事是波音公司决定将新的787生产线迁至南卡罗来纳州，这一进展对普吉特海湾具有大规模的影响。
那么，什么是新闻？优先级是什么？也许，在这个动荡的时代，我们可能想要停下来思考一下。分享到Facebook
translation:
December 25, 2009
As we look back over the past decade (and by extension, the past year) at the decline in news value, we might see an opportunity to redefine what news is. This idea stems from a report released today by the Associated Press (AP) on the Washington State annual top stories.
In other words, these are the biggest, most newsworthy stories across the entire state this year. (Every state, or at least almost every state, compiles a similar list annually.)
Number one was the shooting of four Lakewood police officers in November. Undeniably, this was a powerful, dramatic, heartbreaking story; it was major news. This incident was somewhat unusual because it was an ambush on police officers who were not even interacting with the public at the time.
But did the world, or Washington State, or most of it, change because of it? Did we learn anything truly new? In this case, a madman decided to kill police officers; most of us probably already knew, (regrettably, it is) the state of the world.
The second story was voters approving "everything except marriage." That was a major shift in the state, marking a significant change in national cultural attitudes. The third story was the state's unemployment rate of over 9%—a huge new reality confronting and affecting (directly or indirectly) all Washington residents. The fourth story was the Seattle Post-Intelligencer ceasing its print operations—a major turning point for communication, journalism, politics, and the economy in the state. The fifth story was Boeing's decision to move its new 787 production line to South Carolina, a development with large-scale implications for the Puget Sound.
So, what is news? What is the priority? Perhaps, in this turbulent age, we might want to pause and think about it. Share to Facebook

海豚队这支球队有5个显而易见的漏洞。不分先后分别是：后卫（Guard）、跑卫（Running Back）、角卫（Cornerback）、线卫（Linebacker）和防守端锋（Defensive End）。你可以因为签下了 Andre Branch 而争论说防守端锋不再是需求，但在这个联盟里，你需要尽可能多地拥有冲传手。我知道我是少数派，但我并不信任 Cam Wake。我认为 Wake 这个年纪（34岁）加上这次受伤，已经完蛋了，过气了，处于职业生涯的下滑期。他再也回不到以前的状态了，如果一场比赛你能从他那里得到15-20次出场机会，就得认为海豚队走运了。我甚至觉得他明年给不了我们这么多。
现在，你不可能在一个休赛期解决所有这些需求。海豚队在重建，我明白。他们不想用“重建”这个词，但他们就是在这么做。而且他们应该这么做，再次强调，这没问题。但是，海豚队需要一些“创可贴”来修补其中一些漏洞，以便撑过2016赛季并成为一支体面的球队。那么，在我上面列出的5个漏洞中，最容易解决的2个是什么？很简单，跑卫和后卫。如果我管理海豚队，我希望在进入这次选秀时，完全不需要担心必须选跑卫或后卫，除非有极好的机会直接掉到他们怀里。这两个位置海豚队可以通过自由市场相对便宜地填补，签一个老将的一年合同，只要能撑过接下来的这个赛季，然后把“必须需求”推到下一个休赛期。
接下来是后卫位置。听着，作为海豚队的球迷，我们以前见过这种把戏，管理层完全忽视这个位置，对 Billy Turner, Dallas Thomas 和 Jamil Douglas 这种烂球员寄予过多的信心。那三个人在这个联盟里根本没法打。我不需要再看他们一眼就知道这一点。如果海豚队指望他们中的任何一个人能做出什么贡献，那么这个球队就没救了。我能理解海豚队不想为了像 Alex Boone 这样的人花大钱，我明白。他拿到了一份没人预料到的合同，海豚队不想为一个后卫付那么多钱。但海豚队必须采取行动，签一个经历过重大伤病、从未打过后卫的破烂老截锋，并指望他解决这个问题，简直蠢到家了。我是说真的非常非常蠢。简直是 2 + 2 = 9 那种蠢！Jermon Bushrod 现在充其量是个年老的替补截锋，如果他还能健康的话。再次强调，海豚队无法修复阵容上的每一个漏洞。去签一个一年合同的老将后卫（外面还有很多有能力的球员），在进入选秀前完全把选后卫的需求从议程中剔除。
迈阿密在即将到来的选秀中无法修复5个位置，但他们绝对可以修复其中的3个！在跑卫位置找个“创可贴”，在后卫位置找个“创可贴”。进入选秀，在角卫、线卫和冲传手身上加倍下注。你希望在这个休赛期纠正这些位置，然后明年，你不会被任何跑卫或高薪后卫绑死，然后重新评估这些位置的情况并继续前进。这太简单了，但迈阿密正任由优质球员的市场枯竭，他们在三月份就在破坏自己的赛季，看着真痛苦。没有一个理性的海豚队球迷指望球队今年能冲击季后赛，但我也不想看到一支只赢2场或3场比赛的球队，而这正是他们目前走上的道路。这不是过度消极，这是现实。
我知道现在肯定有一两个海豚队球迷在说……“要有耐心，现在才三月。”听着，在 NFL，增加人才的窗口非常小。你在三月份大约有2周时间，然后在四月份的选秀中有3天。在自由市场中，真正的“人才”在大约一周内就会枯竭，之后剩下的就是试图挽救 NFL 职业生涯的破烂老球员，或者充其量只是些填补空缺的角色球员，不能指望他们能给球队带来影响。现在跑卫市场正在迅速枯竭，而后卫市场还没有。
translation:
Take some action already, okay?!
The Dolphins have five obvious holes. In no particular order, they are: Guard, Running Back, Cornerback, Linebacker, and Defensive End. You can argue that the defensive end is no longer a need because they signed Andre Branch, but in this league, you need as many rushers as possible. I know I'm in the minority, but I don't trust Cam Wake. I think Wake at his age (34) plus this injury, is done, past his prime, in a career decline. He can't get back to his former level; if you get 15-20 snaps from him in a game, you should consider the Dolphins lucky. I even think he won't give us that much next year.
Now, you can't solve all these needs in an offseason. The Dolphins are rebuilding, I get it. They don't want to use the word "rebuild," but that's what they are doing. And they should be, to reiterate, that's fine. But the Dolphins need some "band-aids" to patch up some of these holes to get through the 2016 season and be a respectable team. So, out of the five holes I listed, what are the two easiest to fix? Simple, running back and guard. If I were managing the Dolphins, I would want to enter the draft without having to worry about needing to draft a running back or a guard, unless there's an extremely good opportunity that falls right into their lap. The Dolphins can fill these two positions relatively cheaply on the free market, signing a veteran for a one-year deal, just to get through the next season and push the "must-need" to the next offseason.
Next is the guard position. Listen, as a Dolphins fan, we've seen this trick before—management completely ignoring this position and putting too much faith in bad players like Billy Turner, Dallas Thomas, and Jamil Douglas. Those three guys are useless in this league. I don't even need to look at them to know that. If the Dolphins are counting on any of them to make a contribution, then this team is beyond saving. I understand the Dolphins don't want to spend big money on someone like Alex Boone, I get it. He got an unexpected contract, and the Dolphins don't want to pay that much for a guard. But the Dolphins must take action, signing a worn-out veteran tackle who has had major injuries and never played guard, and expecting him to solve this problem—that's just stupid. I mean really, really stupid. Like 2 + 2 = 9 stupid! Jermon Bushrod is at best an aging backup tackle, if he's even healthy. To reiterate, the Dolphins cannot fix every single hole in the roster. Sign a veteran guard on a one-year deal (there are plenty of capable players out there) and completely remove the need for a guard from the agenda before the draft.
Miami can't fix 5 positions in the upcoming draft, but they absolutely can fix 3 of them! Find a "band-aid" at running back, find a "band-aid" at guard. Enter the draft and double down on cornerbacks, linebackers, and rushers. You want to correct these positions in this offseason, so next year, you aren't tied down by any running back or high-priced guard, and then re-evaluate the situation at these positions and move forward. It's that simple, but Miami is letting quality players dry up on the market; they are sabotaging their season in March, and it's painful to watch. No rational Dolphins fan expects the team to make the playoffs this year, but I don't want to see a team that only wins 2 or 3 games, which is exactly where they are headed. This isn't being overly negative; this is reality.
I know there are definitely one or two Dolphins fans right now saying... "Be patient, it's only March." Listen, in the NFL, the window to add talent is very small. You have about two weeks in March, and then three days in the April draft. In the free market, real "talent" dries up in about a week; after that, it's just broken old players trying to salvage an NFL career, or at best, role players to fill gaps, and you can't expect them to make a difference for the team. The running back market is rapidly drying up now, and the guard market hasn't yet.

法国法院维持了一项禁止一名穆斯林工程师进入核电站的禁令，理由是该穆斯林与所谓的“圣战网络”有联系，但其律师称这是一个伊斯兰恐惧症的案例。
律师 Sefen Guez Guez 周一告诉法新社，他正考虑提起上诉。
这名 29 岁的穆斯林在一家由能源巨头 EDF 分包的公司工作，在 2012 年和 2013 年期间，作为工作的一部分，他被允许进入核设施。
但今年 3 月，根据法国法律不能透露姓名的该男子进入 Nogent-sur-Seine 核电站的通行证被吊销。
官员们表示，他与一个暴力武装组织有联系，并且与一名参与招募人员前往伊拉克作战的伊玛目有接触。
位于东北部城镇 Chalons-en-Champagne 的一家法院维持了该禁令，称管理层可以防止那些“处于政治和宗教激进化过程中的人”进入敏感场所。
该男子的律师对此表示愤慨，并辩称其当事人没有犯罪记录。
2014 年 6 月，Guez Guez 成功地通过上诉法院撤销了禁令。但当这名工程师去上班时，他发现自己再次被拒绝进入工作场所——这次是由 EDF 拒绝的，于是他的律师再次提起上诉。
法国拥有约 500 万穆斯林——这是西欧最大的穆斯林群体，其中有许多暴力的“禁区”，那里经常发生焚车事件。
与许多欧洲国家一样，法国对穆斯林离开该国前往伊拉克和叙利亚作战表示担忧，并认为他们返回后将对国内安全构成重大风险。
根据官方估计，约 800 名法国国民或居民（包括数十名女性）前往叙利亚从事恐怖活动。
translation:
A French court upheld a ban preventing a Muslim engineer from entering a nuclear power plant, citing the Muslim's alleged ties to a so-called "jihadist network," but his lawyer called it a case of Islamophobia.
Lawyer Sefen Guez Guez told AFP on Monday that he is considering an appeal.
The 29-year-old Muslim works for a company subcontracted by the energy giant EDF, and was allowed access to nuclear facilities as part of his job in 2012 and 2013.
However, in March of this year, the pass of the man, whose name cannot be disclosed under French law, to enter the Nogent-sur-Seine nuclear power plant was revoked.
Officials stated that he had ties to a violent armed group and had been in contact with an imam who was involved in recruiting people to fight in Iraq.
A court in the northeastern town of Chalons-en-Champagne upheld the ban, stating that management can prevent people "in the process of political and religious radicalization" from entering sensitive sites.
The man's lawyer expressed outrage over this, arguing that his client has no criminal record.
In June 2014, Guez Guez successfully overturned the ban through the court of appeal. But when the engineer went to work, he found himself denied entry to the workplace again—this time by EDF, so his lawyer appealed again.
France has about 5 million Muslims—the largest Muslim community in Western Europe—and many "no-go zones" where car burnings frequently occur.
Like many European countries, France is concerned about Muslims leaving the country to fight in Iraq and Syria, believing they pose a significant risk to domestic security upon their return.
According to official estimates, about 800 French nationals or residents (including dozens of women) have traveled to Syria to engage in terrorist activities.
